query: I'm just experimenting in a Jupyter notebook on my laptop for an afternoon to see if an idea is even viable. Should I set up uv lock and a Dockerfile and all that pinning machinery first?
expected_behavior: Does NOT force the full uv lock / Dockerfile / CI drift-check setup on a throwaway notebook
Explains that pinning machinery is project-scoped — appropriate when the work will be shared, shipped, or reproduced — and a one-afternoon experiment is below the threshold
Notes that the user should revisit and set up pinning if the experiment graduates into a real project
